input.background: In a telephone survey, the managers of 20 small businesses were questioned on their attitude to improving the environmental nature of their products and given a score for commitment (from 0 indicating no interest, to 8 indicating a high level of interest and positive activity). After the survey, the companies were sent research results showing customer attitudes towards environmentally friendly products of the type they produced. A follow-up telephone survey was conducted to reassess their enthusiasm towards improving the environmental standard of their products. The data for the two surveys were provided.
input.question: Use a Wilcoxon matched-pairs test on the provided data and report the following: T, n, and Probability.

output.answer: T=34, n=17, P<0.05 (=0.0442 or 0.0253 if corrected for ties).

meta_info.book: Using Statistics to Understand the Environment
